title: Read-only visualization layer over committed .brain/ JSON store
context: Engineering Memory OS synthesizes context from git activity into .brain/ JSON files. Need accessible UI for engineers to inspect memory without backend dependency.
decision: VS Code extension reads directly from committed .brain/ JSON, watches for changes on disk, displays invariants/decisions/bugs/symbols/components in tree view. No synthesis or regeneration—pure read layer.
consequences: Extension is fast (no computation, just file reads and tree layout)
Memory state directly auditable in version control—diffs show what changed
Extension must track .brain/ schema changes; breaking schema updates require extension updates
Synthesis process remains single source of truth; extension never modifies .brain/
Users see consistent snapshot (what's committed), not real-time analysis
alternatives_rejected: Extension queries MCP/API server for data (adds latency, runtime coupling)
Extension regenerates analysis from source on demand (expensive per-file analysis)
Make extension read-write (risk of conflicts with synthesis, inconsistent state)
Embed pre-computed snapshots in extension manifests (poor scalability, updates require re-publish)
